# 2026 Member Email

**Subject:** Blockchain in government is moving — join us Fridays

---

Hi everyone,

Quick note as we head into 2026. The ACT-IAC Blockchain Working Group is active and the landscape has shifted meaningfully:

- The **GENIUS Act** is now law — the first major federal crypto legislation
- An **Executive Order** directed an interagency framework for digital assets
- The **Deploying American Blockchains Act** passed the House, directing OMB to develop a federal blockchain strategy
- Five states have enacted Bitcoin reserve or digital asset laws
- Treasury, GAO, and NSF published their **JFMIP blockchain prototype** findings on grants management

This isn't speculative anymore. Agencies are building, legislatures are legislating, and the compliance and oversight questions are real. The working group exists to make sense of it — practically, not theoretically.

**What we maintain:**

- A tracked **legislation watchlist** — federal, state, and international
- A catalog of **federal use cases** — DHS supply chain traceability, IRS crypto enforcement, DoD logistics, FDA drug tracking, and more
- A **sandbox** of technologies the group has evaluated
- An **adoption framework** for inter-agency blockchain pilots
- A **resources library** of standards, reports, and guidelines

All of it is open and on GitHub: https://github.com/blockchain-working-group/agendas

**Three ways to participate:**

1. **Friday mornings, 10:00–10:45 AM ET** — short, low-commitment calls. Propose a topic, share something you're seeing, or just listen. Join at https://meet.google.com/iyg-zqve-wne

2. **Discord** — async conversation between calls. Post a link, ask a question, continue a Friday discussion. Join at https://discord.gg/xq5JsbBv4J

3. **GitHub** — file an issue with a use case, a question, or a speaker suggestion. Comment on what's there. Everything is in the open.

No long-term obligations. Drop in when something is relevant to your work and drop out when it isn't. The meeting is 45 minutes, the Discord is async, and the GitHub is permanent.

If you know someone in government, industry, or academia working on blockchain, digital identity, or public-sector DLT — forward this along. We're stronger with more perspectives in the room.

See you Friday.

— ACT-IAC Blockchain Working Group
